name: Ningbo
tagline: Not a chip town — a components, specialty-materials & Tier-1 auto-electronics powerhouse; overwhelmingly private, port-driven (world's #1 cargo port).
tagline_zh: 并非芯片之城 — 而是元件、特殊材料与汽车电子 Tier-1 重镇;民营主导、依托全球第一大货运港。

clusters.what: China's 'Magnet Capital' — 40%+ of national output; for EV/wind/robot motors. Geopolitically hot (2025 rare-earth export controls).
anchors: Yunsheng (韵升) — ~25% China NEV traction-magnet share

clusters.what: China's densest private Tier-1 cluster; ~5,000 auto-parts firms within 50km; 4 in global top-100.
anchors: Joyson 均胜 (¥55.9B, #2 global passive safety)
Tuopu 拓普 (thermal/NVH, Tesla Optimus actuators)

clusters.what: World #1 in artificial-graphite anode and high-nickel cathode.
1-in-3 Android camera modules; #1 automotive lens.
Relay output 200M+/yr; Guanhaiwei = world's largest European-socket base.
Cixi makes ~60% of the world's small appliances (irons 60%, hair dryers 30%).
Yuyao = China's Plastics City / mold capital; injection-molding machines #1 world.
Major inverter/storage exporters; HJT solar.
Sputtering targets #1 world; OSAT #14 global (Yuyao).

subdistricts.name: Yuyao 余姚
subdistricts.focus: Plastics City + mold capital; Sunny Optical; semi targets

subdistricts.name: Beilun 北仑
subdistricts.focus: Deep-water port; auto-parts parks; magnets; steel

subdistricts.name: Yinzhou 鄞州
subdistricts.focus: HQ district — Yunsheng, Shanshan, Ronbay; auto Tier-1

subdistricts.name: Zhenhai 镇海
subdistricts.focus: Sinopec ZRCC — China's largest refinery (plastics/film feedstock)

valuechain: Sits at the components / specialty-materials / Tier-1 sub-system layer — not chips, not final assembly, not design. Private-enterprise DNA (民企 96.7% of entities, 85% of jobs); export+port-driven.

sell: Automotive MCUs / power semis / SiC-GaN (to Joyson, Tuopu)

note: AI-deep-researched, cross-verified (caught common errors: Delixi=Wenzhou, Vatti=Guangdong, Fuyao=Fujian — not Ningbo).

name: Guangzhou
tagline: China's auto-manufacturing capital + commerce/trade gateway (Canton Fair) + 'World Display Capital' — SOE/JV-led & assembly-oriented, the opposite of Shenzhen's private design ecosystem.
tagline_zh: 中国汽车制造之都 + 贸易门户(广交会)+ '世界显示之都';以国企/合资和整车组装为主,与深圳的民营设计生态相反。

clusters.what: A top auto-output city — ~3.18M vehicles in 2024 (38% NEV), full ICE+hybrid+BEV across six OEM brands; growing car-export hub via Nansha.
'World Display Capital' — TCL CSOT T9 8.6-gen fab (180k sheets/mo, ~¥30B/yr) + buying LG Display's Guangzhou LCD plant. #2 global TV panel.
Deep Tier-1/Tier-2 supplier clusters (Huadu, Panyu) feeding local assembly + export.
Major biopharma cluster — Bio Island, 4,000+ firms, ¥210B revenue.
Pearl-River-mouth refining (smaller than Maoming/Zhanjiang).
GZ's only significant fab — specialty analog/power/display-driver (180–55nm); revenue +53% to ¥2.58B (2025) but deeply loss-making, ChiNext IPO pending.

subdistricts.name: Huangpu / GDD 黄埔
subdistricts.focus: Flagship advanced-mfg zone — ~40% of GZ industrial output; CanSemi fabs, GAC-Honda NEV plant, biopharma

subdistricts.name: Nansha 南沙
subdistricts.focus: Port/FTZ (20.5M TEU, world top-10); GAC-Toyota; petrochem; auto exports +68%

subdistricts.name: Panyu 番禺
subdistricts.focus: GAC Aion EV plant + R&D; XPeng (incl. Aeroht flying-car trial plant)

subdistricts.name: Huadu 花都
subdistricts.focus: Dongfeng Nissan plants + Guangzhou Auto Parts City

valuechain: Assembly + trade-gateway + heavy-industry tier — a net CONSUMER of chips, sensors, power devices and panels (assembles them into cars), not a component originator. More SOE/JV-led than private Shenzhen (GAC, Sinopec, CNOOC, Guangzhou Pharma). Canton Fair = China's largest B2B export-matching platform.

sell: Automotive semis — MCU / power / ADAS / CAN-LIN for GAC's 2M+ vehicles
EV power devices — SiC / IGBT / gate drivers for Aion & XPeng
Display driver ICs + PMIC for CSOT T9
BMS & AI-compute SoC / LiDAR driver for XPeng smart-EV

note: AI-deep-researched (Sonnet), cross-verified — flagged: XPeng mfg split GZ+Zhaoqing; Dongfeng Nissan parent HQ'd in Wuhan; the planned Nansha ethylene cracker was relocated to Zhanjiang.

name: Shanghai
tagline: China's only city with a complete vertically-integrated semiconductor stack — foundry, advanced IC design, materials & equipment — anchored by SMIC & Hua Hong in Zhangjiang; plus Tesla EV.

clusters.what: SMIC (HQ; record 2024 revenue ¥57.8B, +27.7%, ~5.5% global foundry share) + Hua Hong; new Lingang fab JV.
China's #1 IC-design city — ~¥179.5B in 2024 (~27% of national ¥668.9B); hundreds of Zhangjiang design houses.
Growing domestic tool/material firms in Zhangjiang & Lingang bonded zone.
Tesla Gigafactory Shanghai ~950k vehicles/yr (3-millionth Nov 2024); SAIC.

subdistricts.name: Zhangjiang 张江
subdistricts.focus: IC design + SMIC/Hua Hong fabs ('China's Silicon Valley')

subdistricts.name: Lingang 临港
subdistricts.focus: New SMIC fab JV + Tesla Gigafactory; bonded-zone semi incentives

valuechain: Design → fab → packaging at mature-node depth (China leader, 14-28nm); SOE/state-backed at the fab layer (SMIC), private & FDI at design and EMS.

buy: Mature-node logic ICs (28-90nm), MCUs, power & analog

note: Tesla is US-HQ, Foxconn Taiwan-HQ; Hua Hong's leading 12-inch fabs are in Wuxi, not Shanghai.

name: Shenzhen
tagline: China's hardware Silicon Valley — fabless chip design (HiSilicon), finished electronics (DJI), vertically-integrated EV (BYD) and the Huaqiangbei component market coexist; Dongguan assembles what Shenzhen designs.

clusters.what: HiSilicon designs Kirin SoCs & Ascend AI accelerators; >25,000 national hi-tech firms (12/km²).
Foxconn Longhua iPhone OEM (~300k workers); DJI >70% of global consumer drones.
BYD 10-millionth NEV Nov 2024; Shenzhen = 22.3% of China NEV output; in-city cells + IGBT.
Tencent; strategic emerging industries = 42.3% of GDP; R&D 6.46% of GDP (#1 city).
Huaqiangbei — world's largest electronics-component market; same-day BOM fulfilment.

subdistricts.name: Nanshan 南山
subdistricts.focus: Tencent, DJI, Huawei HQ; hi-tech park; VC cluster

valuechain: Design + brand + integration apex of the PRD chain; overwhelmingly private (Huawei/BYD/DJI/Tencent); net exporter of IP-rich finished products.

sell: Smartphones, drones, NEVs, 5G base stations, AI servers, IoT — via ¥4.5T export (#1 city)

note: Foxconn HQ Taiwan (Shenzhen = its largest ops); HiSilicon is a Huawei subsidiary.

name: Beijing
tagline: China's semiconductor R&D + equipment capital (not a volume-fab city) — Naura (#1 domestic equipment), SMIC Beijing fab, the Zhongguancun IC-design cluster, BOE HQ, and the most-permissioned robotaxi zone.

clusters.what: Naura — #6 globally in fab equipment (2024), revenue ¥29.8B (+35%); ~25% of China's etch/deposition WFE.
Haidian IC revenue ¥43B (2024, +13%); GigaDevice = world #2 SPI NOR Flash; Cambricon AI NPUs.
SMIC Beijing FAB4 (Yizhuang) — mature nodes ≥28nm, part of SMIC's 1.2M+ wpm network.
BOE global HQ (management/R&D-heavy; advanced OLED production is in Chengdu/Wuhan).
Yizhuang — Baidu Apollo Go + China's first driverless robotaxi permits (>17M cumulative rides).

subdistricts.name: Haidian / Zhongguancun 海淀
subdistricts.focus: IC design, EDA, AI, university spinouts (Tsinghua, PKU, CAS)

subdistricts.name: Yizhuang / BDA 亦庄
subdistricts.focus: Naura, SMIC Beijing fab, BOE line, Baidu Apollo Park

valuechain: Upstream R&D + equipment layer — private fabless (GigaDevice, Cambricon) + state-backed equipment (Naura) & foundry (SMIC); 'Big Fund' capital.

buy: Silicon wafers, specialty gases, photomasks, packaging substrates

note: Hua Hong has NO Beijing fab (all in Shanghai). BOE's most advanced OLED is in Chengdu/Wuhan, not Beijing.

name: Tianjin
tagline: North China's port-industrial base — automobiles (FAW-Toyota), Airbus A320 final-assembly, petrochemicals and emerging EV/electronics, anchored by Tianjin Port and the Binhai New Area.

clusters.what: FAW-Toyota + FAW heritage; growing NEV assembly in the northern auto belt.
Airbus A320 Final Assembly Line — the first outside Europe; aerospace & rocket plants.
Large refining & chemical base (Nangang petrochemical zone).
Display, EV battery/components and electronics in Binhai/TEDA (Samsung presence reduced).

subdistricts.name: Binhai New Area 滨海新区

valuechain: Heavy-industry + assembly + port logistics; SOE-heavy (FAW, Sinopec, PetroChina) with FDI in autos/aerospace.
note: Curated (lighter than the deep-researched cities) — Tianjin is more heavy-industry/port than electronics; flag for a deeper pass if needed.

name: Suzhou
tagline: China's top FDI-driven electronics & advanced-components belt ('Sunan model') — Samsung, Bosch, ZEISS + thousands of Taiwanese firms in PCB, OSAT, precision components and biomedicine.

clusters.what: Suzhou Industrial Park GDP ¥400B (2024); city industrial output >¥4.7T; high-tech 54.7%.
Major OSAT cluster — Samsung Suzhou DRAM packaging + Taiwanese OSAT.
Kunshan Taiwanese PCB makers; Dongshan Precision (top domestic PCB/precision group).
ZEISS R&D+mfg site (2024); Sodick precision EDM.
BioBAY — China's leading biomedical cluster, >1,200 firms.

valuechain: FDI + Taiwan capital at mid-to-advanced electronics tiers; strong back-end semi + sub-assembly; SOE role limited ('Sunan model').
note: Samsung (KR), ZEISS (DE), Foxconn (TW), ASE (TW), Unimicron (TW) are foreign/Taiwan-HQ.

name: Wuxi
tagline: China's #1 IC packaging/test base + Hua Hong's newest 12-inch specialty foundry + SK hynix's DRAM fab + the nation's sole state-designated IoT cluster.

clusters.what: #1 nationally; Hi-Tech District IC output >¥170B (2024); 500+ IC firms.
Hua Hong Wuxi phase-2 (40nm MCU/eNVM), targeting 83k wpm; STMicro 40nm eNVM transfer.
SK hynix Wuxi DRAM ~180-190k wpm, ~90% on 1a DDR5; 49.9% stake sold to Wuxi state (2024).
China's sole national IoT advanced-mfg cluster; >3,000 IoT firms; ¥401B (2022).

subdistricts.name: Wuxi National Hi-Tech District (Xinwu) 新吴
subdistricts.focus: IC foundry + packaging + IoT; 500+ IC firms

valuechain: Full-stack semiconductor: design (light) → foundry → OSAT → IoT app layer; fab & IoT SOE-guided, packaging large-cap private (Changdian).
note: Hua Hong HQ Shanghai (Wuxi = its largest production base); SK hynix HQ Korea (partial stake now Chinese state).

name: Hefei
tagline: China's boldest 'state-as-VC' city — municipal bets on BOE (display), CXMT (DRAM) and NIO/VW (EV) made it a triple leader in memory, large-panel display and premium EV.

clusters.what: CXMT — China's sole volume DRAM producer; ~160k wpm; ~7.7% global share; DDR5 ramping (~80% yield).
BOE (Hefei-backed since 2008) — LCD G8.5 + WOLED.
NIO (Hefei JV) + VW Anhui (€2.5B); ~1.37M NEV in 2024 (8.3% of China's output).
Midea / Gree Hefei plants.

valuechain: State-directed at every anchor (Hefei gov holds equity in CXMT, NIO, BOE, VW Anhui) — the most interventionist municipal model; operating cos are listed/private/JV.
note: NIO HQ Shanghai; BOE HQ Beijing; VW HQ Germany — Hefei is the manufacturing base + major state investor.

name: Wuhan
tagline: China's only vertically-integrated memory-plus-photonics hub — YMTC's 3D NAND anchors the world's densest fibre-optic / optoelectronics cluster ('Optics Valley').

clusters.what: YMTC ~130k wpm (→200k+ post-Phase III); targeting ~15% global NAND share by 2026.
Optics Valley ~40% of global fibre-preform output; YOFC = global #3 fibre maker.
BOE + TCL CSOT fabs; CSOT t8 Gen-8.6 inkjet-printed OLED (world-first).
>15,000 firms; ¥600B industry (2024); fibre lasers, LiDAR, photonic chips.

subdistricts.name: East Lake Hi-Tech / Optics Valley 光谷

valuechain: Upstream-to-mid in memory (YMTC full-stack NAND) + photonics (preform → cable → device); state-backed, limited FDI post-sanctions.
note: YMTC on US Entity List since 2022; BOE/CSOT are branch fabs (HQ Beijing/Shenzhen); YMTC volumes are analyst estimates.

name: Xi'an
tagline: The only Chinese city pairing Samsung's largest non-Korea NAND complex (~40% of Samsung NAND) with LONGi's world-leading solar campus and a deep aerospace-defence electronics base.

clusters.what: Samsung Xi'an (X1+X2) = ~40% of Samsung's global NAND; ramping 236→286-layer V9.
LONGi HQ — ¥45.2B expansion toward 100GW wafer + 50GW cell capacity.
AVIC Xi'an Aircraft; radiation-hardened ICs for space.
Micron back-end (status uncertain after the 2023 CAC review).

valuechain: Split: Samsung fab = export FDI; LONGi = private global champion; defence/aerospace = SOE with closed supply chains.
note: Samsung HQ Korea (holds a US export exemption); LONGi solar defines Xi'an though it's not classic semiconductor.

name: Chengdu
tagline: China's western EMS-plus-packaging node — Foxconn assembles MacBooks/iPads, Intel runs its China chip-packaging expansion, plus growing IC design and a top gaming hub.

clusters.what: Foxconn (iPads since 2010, MacBooks, Apple Watch); higher-value SKU mix.
Intel Chengdu $300M expansion (server-chip packaging) + customer centre.
China's 'fourth IC city'; AMEC ¥3.05B equipment base; automotive-chip cluster in Tianfu.
Largest gaming hub after Shanghai/Shenzhen (Tencent/NetEase/miHoYo) — drives GPU/datacentre demand.

subdistricts.name: Tianfu New Area 天府新区

valuechain: Mid-to-downstream: final assembly (Foxconn) is largest; Intel packaging adds back-end depth; IC design early-stage; FDI-heavy mfg + private IC/gaming.
note: Foxconn (TW), Intel (US), Pegatron (TW) are branch ops; no confirmed UMC Chengdu fab found.

name: Chongqing
tagline: World's largest laptop-production city (~30% of global notebooks, #1 for 11+ years), now layering NEV auto-electronics and display on its EMS base.

clusters.what: ~80M laptops + 100M+ smart terminals/yr; ~30% of global notebooks; HP/Dell/Lenovo/Acer/Asus.
2.54M vehicles (2024), 953k NEV (+90.5%); #3 in China.
SK hynix Chongqing back-end packaging plant.
BOE line; part of the Chengdu-Chongqing display corridor.

valuechain: Final-assembly + packaging (FDI Taiwanese ODMs for laptops, Korean back-end for memory); auto shifting toward a domestic chain; rail-logistics edge.
note: Quanta/Inventec/Foxconn (TW), SK hynix (KR) are branch ops; HP/Dell are brand clients, not manufacturers.

name: Dongguan
tagline: The PRD's mass-production backbone — ~32 specialized towns; Chang'an outputs ~195 phones/minute and the city makes ~1 in 5 of the world's mobile phones; competes on tooling speed + supplier density, not brands or chip IP.

clusters.what: Chang'an electronics output ¥232.6B (2024); ~1/5 of world phones since 1995.
One of China's 3 biggest PCB hubs (Houjie/Hengli/Dalang); linked to Shenzhen design houses.
Cell-to-pack for phones/TWS/power tools (cells from Shenzhen/Huizhou).
Chang'an/Houjie mold cluster (~¥50B); molds exported globally.

valuechain: Classic Tier-1/2 OEM — converts Shenzhen designs/components into finished assemblies; private SME-heavy, thin margins, diversifying to smart-manufacturing.

sell: Assembled phones, TWS, power-tool packs, PCBA — to Shenzhen brands / export

note: OPPO/vivo are anchored in Chang'an (BBK group); the '1/5 of world phones' figure is order-of-magnitude.

name: Foshan
tagline: The PRD's appliance + industrial-materials heartland — Shunde houses Midea (appliances + KUKA robotics) and Galanz, the largest white-goods concentration on earth; Foshan makes what goes inside homes.

clusters.what: Shunde output ~¥370B; 25% of global rice cookers, 48% of microwaves; Midea revenue ¥409B (2024).
Midea-KUKA Shunde park — China's largest industrial-robot base (>80,000 delivered).
~54% of China / ~25% of world output; 350+ makers, ~1.2B m²/yr.
Nanhai — China's leading architectural aluminum-extrusion hub.

valuechain: Mid-to-finished consumer durables + construction materials; heavily private (Midea/Galanz); Midea vertically integrated (makes own compressors/motors/chips).
note: KUKA HQ Germany (Shunde = mfg base); Hisense HQ Qingdao (has Foshan plants, not Foshan-HQ).

name: Ningde
tagline: The world's most concentrated single-city battery ecosystem — a purpose-built battery capital around CATL where upstream materials, cell production and recycling co-locate; >¥300B output.

clusters.what: CATL HQ — world #1 EV battery 9 years running, 39.2% global share (2025); 661 GWh sold.
>90 upstream/downstream firms; >60% local materials (cathode/anode/separator/electrolyte).
CATL ESS shipments 36 GWh in Q3 2025; Tener / Shenxing cells.
BRUNP (CATL) recycles end-of-life cells into precursors.

valuechain: Almost entirely private (CATL, Zeng Yuqun-controlled); cell + materials manufacturing; sells cells to global OEMs; very little SOE presence.
note: CATL also has fabs in Liyang/Qingdao/Yichun etc.; the '330 GWh' figure is group-wide, not Ningde-only.

name: Changzhou
tagline: The most complete EV new-energy supply chain of any city outside Shenzhen — CATL, SVOLT, CALB (cells), Li Auto (assembly) and Trina Solar (PV) all manufacture here.

clusters.what: Three makers in parallel — CATL Liyang, SVOLT (HQ Jintan), CALB (HQ Changzhou); Jintan alone 108.5 GWh (~20% of China).
Li Auto main plant (Wujin); city NEV output 678k (2023) → 800k+ (2024).
Trina Solar HQ — top-5 module maker; 2024 revenue $11.27B; H1 2024 34GW shipped.
Deep Tier-1/2 — electrolyte, thermal, BMS, power electronics; new-energy output ¥768B (2023).

subdistricts.name: Jintan 金坛
subdistricts.focus: SVOLT HQ — largest Jiangsu battery base

subdistricts.name: Xinbei 新北
subdistricts.focus: Trina Solar HQ; PV module production

valuechain: Mixed SOE/private; mid-stream cell manufacturing + finished assembly; sources materials regionally, exports via the Yangtze River Delta.
note: Li Auto HQ Beijing (mfg in Changzhou); CALB relocated from Luoyang (2015); CATL HQ Ningde.
